name: Adolescent Brain Cognitive Development Study (ABCD)
description: The largest long-term longitudinal study of brain development and child health in the United States, following 11,878 children recruited at ages 9–10 from 21 sites. Biennial multimodal MRI (structural, resting-state and task fMRI, diffusion) is paired with deep phenotyping of cognition, mental health, social relationships, culture/environment, screen and social media use, and peer networks — a rich substrate for developmental social neuroscience.
citation: Casey BJ, Cannonier T, Conley MI, et al. (2018). The Adolescent Brain Cognitive Development (ABCD) study: Imaging acquisition across 21 sites. Developmental Cognitive Neuroscience, 32, 43–54.

publications.title: The ABCD study: understanding the development of risk for mental and physical health outcomes
